content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria put the eggs and vegetables into a large ___ and mixed everything together.
distractor_rationale: a plate is flat and cannot hold a mixture for mixing — you mix ingredients in a deep container
a cup is too small and narrow to mix eggs and vegetables together
a glass is a drinking vessel and cannot be used to mix food ingredients

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom spread ___ on his bread because it melts into the warm surface and makes it taste rich.
distractor_rationale: oil is a liquid and cannot be spread on bread in the same way — you do not spread oil, you drizzle it
honey does not melt into bread the way butter does — it stays sticky on the surface
jam does not melt into bread — it sits on top as a cold layer

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She put the pot of water on the stove and waited for it to ___ before adding the pasta.
distractor_rationale: water does not freeze on a stove — freezing requires very low temperatures, not heat
water does not melt — melting applies to solids, not liquids
water cannot burn — burning requires a combustible material, not water

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ at the restaurant trained for ten years and now creates all the menus and prepares every dish in the kitchen.
distractor_rationale: a waiter serves food to tables but does not create menus or prepare dishes in the kitchen
a cashier handles payments and does not prepare food or design menus
a customer eats at the restaurant but does not work in the kitchen or create menus

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Anna grilled the ___ breasts with herbs and lemon juice for twenty minutes until they were fully cooked through.
distractor_rationale: beef does not come in 'breasts' — this body-part cut specifically refers to poultry such as chicken
beans are small legumes that have no breast cut and are not grilled as a whole piece
cheese does not have a breast cut and is not grilled for twenty minutes in this way

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The birthday ___ had three layers of sponge with cream between each one, and it was decorated with fresh strawberries on top.
distractor_rationale: a biscuit is a single flat, thin, dry item — it cannot have three layers with cream between them
a chip is a thin fried slice of potato — it cannot have layers of sponge or cream
a dish is a general word for any food item or a container — it does not have layers of sponge or birthday decoration

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: David added grated ___ to the top of the pasta and it melted into the hot sauce, making it creamy and salty.
distractor_rationale: chocolate is sweet and is not grated onto pasta — it would create a sweet, not salty, flavour
butter is not grated — it is a soft solid that is cut or spread, not grated into shreds
cream is a liquid and cannot be grated — grating applies only to solid foods

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The slow-cooked ___ stew was made with thick slices of red meat, carrots, and red wine, and it simmered on the stove for three hours.
distractor_rationale: chicken is white meat, not red meat — the sentence specifically states 'thick slices of red meat', which rules out chicken
beans are small legumes and cannot be described as 'thick slices of red meat'
chocolate is a sweet ingredient and is never described as 'thick slices of red meat' in a savoury stew

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Everyone at the dinner table agreed that the lamb ___ was ___ — the spices were perfectly balanced and the meat was incredibly tender.
distractor_rationale: raw means uncooked, which contradicts the description of perfectly balanced spices and tender meat that has clearly been cooked
frozen means not yet cooked or thawed, which contradicts serving it at a dinner table as a finished meal
salty describes only one flavour quality and contradicts 'perfectly balanced spices', which implies a range of well-combined flavours

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Lisa soaked the dried ___ in cold water overnight so that they would soften enough to cook into the vegetable soup the next morning.
distractor_rationale: biscuits are dry baked goods that dissolve when soaked in water and are never soaked overnight to prepare for soup
chips are already cooked fried potato slices and are never soaked overnight to soften them for soup
cream is a liquid dairy product that cannot be soaked in water — it is already a liquid and requires no softening

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria put the eggs and vegetables into a large ___ and mixed them together.
distractor_rationale: a dish is a flat container used for serving food, not for mixing ingredients together
a chip is a thin fried slice of potato, not a container
a bean is a type of food, not a container used for mixing

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom put the pan of water on the stove and waited for it to ___ before adding the pasta.
distractor_rationale: butter is a solid fat spread, not an action a pan of water can perform
cream is a dairy product, not an action that water performs on a stove
a biscuit is a baked food item, not an action that water performs

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The restaurant's most popular meal is a slow-cooked ___ stew made with carrots, onions, and red wine.
distractor_rationale: chocolate is a sweet ingredient and cannot be described as slow-cooked in a stew with carrots and red wine
a biscuit is a baked snack and is never used as the main ingredient in a stew
cake is a sweet baked dessert and cannot be cooked in a stew with carrots and red wine

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Everyone at the dinner table agreed that the homemade soup was absolutely ___, and they all asked for a second helping.
distractor_rationale: boiled describes a cooking method, not a positive judgment about overall taste quality
creamy describes one specific texture quality and cannot express the general approval shown by asking for a second helping
cheesy describes a single flavour characteristic and does not convey the universal agreement about the soup's quality

content.instruction: Choose the correct word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The head ___ at the hotel trained for ten years in professional kitchens before designing the restaurant's new menu.
distractor_rationale: a customer visits a restaurant to eat and does not train in professional kitchens or design menus
a waiter serves food to customers and does not train in kitchens or design menus
a farmer grows ingredients but does not train in professional kitchens or create restaurant menus

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She put the pasta in a large ___ of hot water to cook it.
distractor_rationale: a plate is flat and cannot hold water for cooking pasta
a cup is too small to cook pasta in
a glass is a drinking vessel and cannot be used to cook pasta on a stove

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom spread ___ on his bread because it melts quickly when the bread is warm.
distractor_rationale: cheese does not melt on warm bread when simply spread — it must be heated separately
cream is a liquid and cannot be spread on bread in the same way
chocolate does not melt on warm bread at room temperature in normal conditions

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: You need to ___ the eggs in water for ten minutes to make them hard.
distractor_rationale: baking uses dry oven heat, not water, so it cannot make hard-boiled eggs
frying uses oil in a pan, not water, and does not produce hard eggs in ten minutes
grilling uses direct dry heat and cannot cook eggs submerged in water

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The birthday ___ had three layers of sponge with cream and strawberries between each one.
distractor_rationale: a biscuit is a single flat, thin item and cannot have multiple layers with cream
a chip is a thin slice of fried potato and cannot have layers of sponge and cream
a dish refers to a type of food or a container, not a layered baked dessert with sponge

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ at the restaurant trained for years to prepare complex sauces and grilled meats.
distractor_rationale: a waiter serves food to tables but does not prepare sauces or cook meats
a customer eats the food and does not work in the kitchen preparing dishes
a cashier handles payments at the till and does not train to cook complex dishes

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Anna added kidney ___ to the stew because they are high in protein and make the dish more filling.
distractor_rationale: chips are fried potato slices and are not added to stew as a protein source
biscuits are dry baked snacks and are not added to stew for protein
cream is a dairy liquid added for richness, not for protein, and is not described as 'kidney'

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The soup was so ___ that everyone at the table asked for a second serving.
distractor_rationale: raw means uncooked, which contradicts the context of a soup being served at a table
frozen means not yet thawed or cooked, which contradicts a hot soup being served
salty describes a specific unpleasant excess of salt, which would not cause everyone to ask for more

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The recipe called for minced ___ as the main ingredient, so Lisa bought a 500-gram pack from the butcher.
distractor_rationale: cheese is not sold in minced form at a butcher's shop and is not a meat ingredient
butter is a dairy fat, not a meat product sold by a butcher in minced form
chocolate is a sweet ingredient that is not sold at a butcher's and cannot be minced as a main savoury ingredient

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The traditional ___ in that region is made by slowly roasting whole pieces of meat with root vegetables for several hours.
distractor_rationale: a bean is a single ingredient, not a complete traditional preparation involving roasting meat and vegetables
a biscuit is a small dry baked item and cannot describe a slow-roasted meat preparation
a chip is a thin fried slice of potato and cannot refer to a complete regional culinary preparation

content.instruction: Choose the best word to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The sauce was made by heating ___ gently in a pan until it thickened into a rich, smooth coating for the dessert.
distractor_rationale: beef is a solid meat and cannot be heated to thicken into a smooth coating for a dessert
chicken is a solid meat that does not thicken into a smooth sauce when heated and is not used as a dessert coating
a bean is a solid legume that does not melt or thicken into a smooth liquid coating when gently heated

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: This soup is very ___. I want more! (DELIGHT)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: She put the vegetables in a pot of ___ water to cook them quickly. (BOIL)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The ___ of the chocolate cake filled the whole kitchen with a wonderful smell. (BAKE)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The restaurant is well known for its ___ beef dishes, which are slow-cooked for several hours. (CREAM)

content.instruction: Use the word in brackets in the correct form to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The head chef gave clear ___ to the kitchen team about how to prepare each dish before the dinner service began. (INSTRUCT)
